nodes.description: Behavior-preserving code simplification and readability-focused refactoring skill
Executable prompt specification with explicit phases: understand-before-edit, simplification candidate selection, incremental transformation, verification, final review
Five principles for code simplification: Preserve Behavior Exactly, Follow Project Conventions, Prefer Clarity Over Cleverness, Maintain Balance, Scope to What Changed
Principle 1: Don't change what code does - only how it expresses it
Principle 2: Make code more consistent with codebase, not impose external preferences
Principle 3: Explicit code is better than compact code when compact version requires mental pause to parse
Principle 4: Watch for over-simplification - don't inline away meaningful names, don't merge unrelated logic
Principle 5: Default to simplifying recently modified code, avoid unrelated drive-by refactors
